rulings.Case Title: PETER H GOSS VS. MARGARET SCHEINMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 09, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS MARGARET SCHEINMAN AND MELVIN SCHEINMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. There are triable issues of fact as to the first three causes of action for misrepresentation. The testimony as to how the option is to be calculated is not barred by the parole evidence rule on the tort claims. The statements allegedly were made to induce plaintiffs to enter into the contract; the statements are not contrary to the lease terms but are supplemental; and defendants have not addressed the alleged discrepancy in the size of the property. The motion is granted as to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract, which is an integrated agreement. As such, there is simply an agreement to agree and no breach based on the decision in Etco Corp. v. Hauer (1984) 161 Cal.App.3d 1154. Even if the parole testimony was admitted, the agreement remains uncertain for it would require the parties to agree on the market rate. For this reason as well, the motion is granted as to the seventh cause of action for reformation and the eighth cause of action for injunctive relief. The motion is denied as to the 5th cause of action because defendants failed to shift the burden of proof. Defendants moving papers do not address the negotiating issues raised by plaintiffs that are alleged to be a basis for the fifth cause of action, including the square footage. The motion is denied as to the 6th cause of action. There is a remaining dispute between the parties. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

rulings.Case Title: MONTGOMERY-WASHINGTON HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 09, 2008 LINE 13. RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT AND CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings IS DENIED. THE COURT MAY TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE OF THE CHIPPING DECLARATION, BUT IT IS PROBLEMATIC TO ACCEPT AS TRUE EACH FACT IN THE DECLARATION. MOREOVER, DEFENDANT SEEKS TO HAVE THE COURT INTERPRET THE DECLARATION TO PRECLUDE THIS CASE FROM GOING FORWARD. THE ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT ARE BEST ADDRESSED BY A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN OPPOSITION TO THE WRIT SO THE COURT MAY CONSIDER ALL RELEVANT EVIDENCE ON THE ISSUE OF ABILITY OF PLAINTIFF TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION. =(302/PJM/PB)
